prompt: Develop a DojoStay Operations & Community system for martial arts organizations that need offline-first training coordination plus on-premise lodging-style room availability for visiting athletes and events. The Vue.js web interface supports four roles—Customer/Student, Photographer, Organization Staff, and Administrator—each with tailored navigation and dashboards. Students maintain a profile with enrollment status and organization master data (school, program, class group, and housing assignment), with inline validation and clear error feedback during edits and bulk imports using downloadable templates and post-import error reports. For engagement, users can post comments with replies and quotes on booking pages and student profiles, like/favorite content, follow users, and @mention others; an in-app notification center shows unread counts and allows muting threads or blocking users, while moderation tools let Staff hide or restore flagged comments. Sparring/partner training booking provides configurable time slots in 30-minute increments, venue versus online session type, and matching filters such as level (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced), weight class in lbs, and style; the UI clearly shows conflicts before submission and guides the user through an order lifecycle from Initiated to Confirmed, Canceled, or Refunded (refunds are internal credits only, no online payments). Property details and availability display presents facility basics, amenities, images, and policies (for example, check-in after 3:00 PM, $100 deposit, children allowed with a guardian, no pets), and shows room-type comparison with a calendar view of nightly rates and availability changes by date. The backend uses Spring Boot to implement RESTful APIs consumed by the decoupled Vue.js frontend over the local network, with MySQL as the system-of-record for users, roles, permissions, bookings, availability calendars, comments, notifications, and audit trails. Authentication is strictly offline with username and password, supporting JWT or server sessions; passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, stored with salted hashing, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts, with admin unlock. Authorization follows a three-layer role-permission-resource model, plus data-scope permissions that restrict records by organization, department, or facility area; export actions require explicit permission and apply a visible watermark including username and timestamp. Identity/credential review is handled fully offline: Staff can review uploaded credential files (type/size checks, SHA-256 fingerprinting to detect duplicates) and approve, reject with reasons, or blacklist accounts under risk-control policies; all moderation and access changes write immutable operation logs. Sensitive fields such as ID numbers and phone numbers are encrypted at rest and masked on display, and the API enforces rate limiting, CSRF protection, and SQL injection defenses. Operational readiness includes centralized logs, metrics, and trace correlation within the deployment, health checks, configuration-change auditing, scheduled backups with point-in-time restore testing, anomaly alerts based on local thresholds, and controlled chaos drills plus one-click degradation toggles (for example, temporarily disabling community features while keeping booking read-only) to maintain availability in constrained environments.
